page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd is a high-density server instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9B14 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 virtual CPUs across 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled, paired with 720 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 6432 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 16 drives, with no integrated GPU resources.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. It achieves top-tier ratings in multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU testing, and memory bandwidth, with read speeds reaching up to 345.79 GB/sec for large blocks. Cryptographic and vector execution are supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and AES instructions. The local NVMe storage configuration supports high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a high-density hardware profile that consolidates massive compute and storage resources into a single node. This density reduces the overhead of managing multiple smaller instances for large workloads, though it represents a significant resource commitment. A notable tradeoff is the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration, meaning parallel processing tasks like deep learning training must rely on CPU-based execution, which, while strong in LLM inference benchmarks, may not match GPU-dedicated alternatives.

The c3d-standard-180-lssd is designed for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180-lssd is a high-density server configured with 180 vCPUs from dedicated AMD EPYC 9B14 processors and 720 GB of RAM. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance integrates 6432 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives, with no GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density for consolidating large workloads, offsetting the lack of GPU acceleration with massive CPU and storage throughput. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density x86_64 compute platform combining 180 virtual cores with 6.4 terabytes of integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 180 vCPUs and 720 GB RAM
Includes 6432 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16 instruction sets
Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
